benchmark_report.title: 🔬 SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKING REPORT 2025-08-25
benchmark_report.subtitle: Your commands have been scientifically roasted

executive_summary.most_shocking_finding: reasoning-wrapper.md has 20% success rate - that's not intelligent, that's gambling with worse odds than a slot machine
executive_summary.biggest_disappointment: Commands average 12 seconds execution - slower than manual work for most tasks
executive_summary.silver_lining: At least they're consistently mediocre (low variance)

savage_commentary: 472 lines of XML hell that somehow works 80% of the time
11.12 seconds average - I can think faster manually without the XML ceremony
No MCP dependencies means it's actually isolated, which is both a feature and a limitation
Coefficient of variation of 3.4% shows it's at least consistently slow
ultrathink-pure-xml.md.the_verdict: Like a Swiss watch that's always 10 seconds behind - reliable but not fast enough for reality
ultrathink-pure-xml.md.improvement_needed: Cut the XML bloat by 60% and focus on the core thinking process

savage_commentary: 60% success rate for a META-COMMAND that's supposed to generate other commands
9.6% coefficient of variation - more inconsistent than my ex's mood swings
At 137 lines, it's supposed to be simple, but fails 2 out of 5 times
Most efficient in tokens/time ratio, but that's like being the smartest kid in remedial math
generate-thinking-command.md.the_verdict: A command generator that fails to generate working commands 40% of the time. That's not meta, that's broken.
generate-thinking-command.md.improvement_needed: Fix the core logic before adding more abstraction layers

savage_commentary: 438 lines to reflect on your day - by the time it finishes, it's tomorrow
14.1 seconds average with 60% success rate - even ADHD folks need reliability
Most variance in execution time (7.9%) - as scattered as the minds it's trying to help
Complex MCP integration that fails 40% of the time defeats the purpose
adhd-evening-reflect.md.the_verdict: An ADHD tool that's more scattered than the condition it's treating. Irony level: maximum.
adhd-evening-reflect.md.improvement_needed: Simplify the reflection process - complex systems don't help ADHD brains

savage_commentary: 🚨 ALERT: 20% success rate is not 'intelligent reasoning', it's broken
415 lines of meta-abstraction hell that fails 4 out of 5 times
Most expensive in tokens (1610) with worst success rate - perfect storm of uselessness
6 abstraction layers - congratulations, you've created a meta-meta-disaster
reasoning-wrapper.md.the_verdict: A reasoning wrapper that can't reason about its own failures. Meta-irony achieved.
reasoning-wrapper.md.improvement_needed: DELETE. Start over. This is not salvageable.

savage_commentary: 365 lines to break down tasks - the irony of needing task breakdown for task breakdown
80% success rate saves it from F-tier, but 12.4 seconds is still slower than manual planning
TASTE method implementation is sound, execution is just bloated
Best token efficiency among the working commands - faint praise
adhd-task-breakdown.md.the_verdict: Actually useful when it works, just wish it worked faster and more reliably
adhd-task-breakdown.md.improvement_needed: Streamline the TASTE implementation - cut 40% of the complexity

speed_rankings.speed_champion_note: Being fastest doesn't matter when you fail 40% of the time

reliability_rankings.reliability_crisis: Only 2 out of 5 commands have acceptable (80%+) success rates

efficiency_analysis.efficiency_note: Token efficiency means nothing if the command doesn't work

statistical_findings.success_rate_crisis: 3 out of 5 commands have sub-80% success rates
statistical_findings.variance_analysis: Low execution variance suggests consistent performance, just consistently mediocre

immediate_actions: 🚨 Fix generate-thinking-command.md success rate before using it
⚡ Speed optimization needed across all commands
🎯 Simplify adhd-evening-reflect.md - complexity kills ADHD usability
architectural_changes: Reduce average line count by 50% - brevity is the soul of wit
Eliminate meta-abstractions unless they provide clear value
Add proper error handling - 60% average success rate is unacceptable
Consider breaking complex commands into smaller, focused utilities
quality_gates: Minimum 85% success rate for any command
Maximum 8-second execution time for simple commands
Token consumption should correlate with actual value provided

final_verdict.grade_explanation: Barely passing due to 2 commands that actually work reliably
final_verdict.most_concerning_trend: High complexity with low reliability - the worst possible combination
final_verdict.path_forward: Radical simplification required. Start with working commands, not clever abstractions.
final_verdict.motivational_insult: Your commands have the reliability of a chocolate teapot and the speed of continental drift. But hey, at least they're consistently disappointing! 🔬
